content.introduction: Quick sort partitions data around a pivot and recursively sorts subarrays, often delivering excellent practical performance.

sections.title: 7.5.1 Key Ideas and Model
sections.content: Define partition invariant and pivot role in separating less-than and greater-than regions.
examples: Explain pivot-choice impact on partition quality
Contrast Hoare and Lomuto partition behavior

sections.title: 7.5.2 Representation and Workflow
sections.content: Describe partition schemes (Lomuto/Hoare), recursive decomposition, and tail recursion optimization ideas.
examples: Record pivot final index after partition
Apply recursion on left and right segments

sections.title: 7.5.3 Complexity and Practical Guidance
sections.content: Average complexity is O(n log n), worst-case O(n^2), with O(log n) expected recursion stack depth.
examples: Use randomized pivot to reduce worst-case risk
Switch to insertion sort on tiny partitions
Choose quick sort for fast in-memory general sorting

keyTakeaways: Balance average efficiency and worst-case risk
Use pivot strategies to improve stability of runtime
Apply quick sort effectively in practice
